g.data-narration: JDBC Database Connection - the essential first step in building data-driven Java applications.
g.text: Learn the 7 steps to connect Java applications to databases

g.data-narration: JDBC is Java's standard API for database connectivity, acting as a bridge between Java apps and database management systems.
g.text: JDBC (Java Database Connectivity) is an API that enables Java applications to interact with
relational databases. It provides standard interfaces for connecting to databases, executing
SQL queries, and processing results. Part of java.sql and javax.sql packages.

g.data-narration: Understanding JDBC is crucial because database-driven apps are fundamental, and this is the most frequently asked topic in exams.
g.text: • Foundation of database programming in Java

g.data-narration: JDBC architecture has four key components: the API, Driver Manager, JDBC Driver, and the Database itself.
There are 4 types of JDBC drivers. Type 4 Thin Driver is most common - pure Java, platform-independent, best performance.
Step 1: Import JDBC classes from java.sql package. Step 2: Load and register the driver using Class.forName.
Step 3: Use DriverManager.getConnection() with URL, username, and password to establish database connection.
Step 4: Create Statement. Step 5: Execute SQL query. Step 6: Process ResultSet to retrieve data.
Step 7: Always close resources in reverse order - ResultSet, Statement, then Connection to prevent memory leaks.
g.text: ⚠️ Always close to prevent memory leaks!

g.data-narration: JDBC URL format varies by database: jdbc:mysql://host:port/db for MySQL, jdbc:postgresql:// for PostgreSQL, jdbc:oracle:thin:@ for Oracle.
The Connection interface provides methods for creating statements, transaction management, and metadata retrieval.
Connection pooling reuses database connections instead of creating new ones, dramatically improving application performance.
g.text: Reuse connections from pool → Execute → Return
Popular pools: HikariCP (fastest), Apache DBCP, C3P0

g.data-narration: Best practices: always close resources, use try-with-resources, connection pooling, externalize config, use PreparedStatement.
Summary: 7 steps to connect - Import, Load Driver, Get Connection, Create Statement, Execute, Process, Close. Remember Type 4 driver and try-with-resources.
g.text: ⭐ Type 4 (Thin) Driver is most common | JDBC 4.0+ auto-loads driver | Use try-with-resources | URL: jdbc:subprotocol://host:port/db
